text: Block periodization and traditional periodization produce equivalent performance improvements in time-trial power over 12 weeks when total training load is matched in trained cyclists.
Both block and traditional periodization improved 5-min and 40-min time-trial power by approximately 8-9% over 12 weeks in trained cyclists.
Block periodization increased red blood cell volume 10% more than traditional periodization over 12 weeks, suggesting different hematological adaptation pathways.
Traditional periodization with progressive cyclic loading increased capillaries around type I muscle fibers 20% more than block periodization over 12 weeks.
specific_value: 20±12% greater capillary increase around type I fibers

text: Power output at 4 mmol/L blood lactate threshold improved by approximately 10% over 12 weeks of structured periodized training in trained cyclists, regardless of block or traditional organization.
VO2peak remained unaltered after 12 weeks of either block or traditional periodized training in already-trained cyclists, suggesting performance gains came from other adaptations.

Block and traditional periodization may produce different muscular capillarization adaptations — block periodization increases capillary density while traditional periodization increases capillary-to-fiber ratio and capillaries around type I fibers.
specific_value: BP: +28±24% capillary density; TP: +36±15% capillary-to-fiber ratio, +17±8% CAF type I
